PROOF OF SERVICE BY MAIL OF DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT, AND FOR RECOVERY OF DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS; MEMO OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT; DECLARATIONS IN SUPPORT; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT; NOTICE OF PAYMENT OF COURT REPORTER'S FEE; [PROPOSED] ORDER. (TRANSACTION ID # 100037956) FILED BY DEFENDANT FISTER, RYAN FISTER, SUNSHINE

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANTS RYAN AND SUNSHINE FISTER'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS MARC BORACCHIA AND LEA WIVIOTT-BORACCHIA IS DENIED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR FEES IS ALSO DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARISE FROM ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SATISFIED THEIR SECOND PRONG BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THERE IS MINIMAL MERIT TO EACH OF THEIR CLAIMS. PER THE REVERSE SUMMARY JUDGMENT STANDARD USED TO EVALUATE WHETHER THE SECOND PRONG BURDEN HAS BEEN MET, PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN THAT THERE IS EVIDENCE WHICH A REASONABLE TRIER OF FACT COULD FIND THAT ONE OR BOTH OF THE ALLEGEDLY FALSE STATEMENTS ATTRIBUTED TO DEFENDANTS WERE PROVABLY FALSE AND THAT DEFENDANTS MADE THOSE STATEMENTS WITH MALICE WHICH DEFEATS THE QUALIFIED COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS MET THEIR SECOND PRONG BURDEN THAT THEIR CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE BECAUSE THE ALLEGEDLY FALSE STATEMENTS ATTRIBUTED TO DEFENDANTS WERE NOT MADE PRELIMINARY TO OR DURING A JUDICIAL OR QUASI-JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN THIS MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS, THEIR FEES REQUEST IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. James A. Bryant II of the Cochran Firm, Tel. No. 1-323-435-8205 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Seth Wiener of the Internet Law Group, Tel. No. 1-310-910-1496 appearing for
the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Ryan and Sunshine Fister's anti-SLAPP motion to strike both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Marc Boracchia and Lea Wiviott-Boracchia is denied as to both causes of action and plaintiffs' request for fees is also denied. Assuming without deciding that plaintiffs' claims arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute, plaintiffs have satisfied their second prong burden of showing that there is minimal merit to each of their claims. Per the reverse summary judgment standard used to evaluate whether the second prong burden has been met, plaintiffs have shown that there is evidence which a reasonable trier of fact could find that one or both of the allegedly false statements attributed to defendants were provably false and that defendants made those statements with malice which defeats the qualified common interest privilege. Moreover, plaintiffs met their second prong burden that their claims are not barred by the litigation privilege because the allegedly false statements attributed to defendants were not made preliminary to or during a judicial or quasi-judicial proceeding. Because plaintiffs have not shown this motion is frivolous, their fees request is denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
